Anthony — known as 'Tony' — is Sam Talcott Hooker's first cousin once removed (colloquially 'uncle'). Tony graduated US Naval Academy B.S. 1961 + San Francisco Art Institute M.F.A. 1995; Principal/Portfolio Manager at Ashfield & Co./Ashfield Capital Partners 1986-present. Tony has been a major source for the Hooker family pedigree.

narrative_blocks.header: Named after his telescope-donor great-uncle
narrative_blocks.body: He was named for his father's uncle John Daggett Hooker, who had built the iron-and-oil fortune that funded the 100-inch Hooker Telescope at Mount Wilson. Tony Hooker preserves a photo with his father's inscription: 'uncle of my father, after whom I was named.'

narrative_blocks.header: Harvard A.B. 1925; Hooker & Fay senior partner
narrative_blocks.body: After Harvard A.B. 1925 he became senior partner of Hooker & Fay brokerage at 340 Pine Street San Francisco in 1929 — just before the October crash. He chaired the SF Stock Exchange Finance Committee in 1948-1949.

narrative_blocks.header: Sailed transatlantic with wife Josephine multiple times
narrative_blocks.body: He and his second wife Josephine Dunham made multiple transatlantic voyages between 1934 and 1955, sailing the SS Santa Rosa, the Queen Mary, and others. He lived through Hillsborough, San Mateo, and Sonoma before his 1983 death in Riverside.

narrative_blocks.header: Father of Anthony 'Tony' Shreve Hooker
narrative_blocks.body: His only son Anthony Shreve Hooker, born 1938, became Principal and Portfolio Manager at Ashfield Capital Partners and the primary contemporary documentor of the Hooker pedigree. Tony's middle name carries forward his maternal grandfather Shreve's family — founders of San Francisco's oldest commercial establishment.

education.notes: Per Golden Nugget Library: graduated 1921. Member of Alumni Association; later President of the Board of Trustees of his alma mater. The Santa Barbara School was a college-preparatory boys' school of the era.

Per Golden Nugget Library: A.B. 1925. The 1921 RMS Aquitania voyage to Europe (London → Italy → Switzerland) was Harvard summer or pre-matriculation — NOT a Yale connection as I previously inferred from the family pattern of his Yale-bound great-uncle Laurence Whitney Hooker.

careers.notes: Per Golden Nugget Library: senior partner of Hooker & Fay since 1929. Offices at 340 Pine Street, San Francisco; also 1429 Stockton Street SF; 352 Village Lane Los Gatos CA; and 420 South Main Street Salinas CA — a multi-office brokerage operation across central and northern California.

Per Golden Nugget Library: Chairman of finance committee for years 1948 and 1949. The SF Stock Exchange operated as the Pacific Coast's primary regional exchange before its 1957 merger with the Los Angeles Stock Exchange to form the Pacific Coast Stock Exchange.

narrative_blocks.header: Daughter of Armour & Co Vice-President
narrative_blocks.body: Josephine Dunham was the daughter of Robert James Dunham (1876-1948), Vice-President of Armour and Company, Director of the Continental and Commercial National Bank, and an officer or director of numerous Chicago corporations (per the 1919 Chicago Blue Book). Family residence was 208 South LaSalle Street, Chicago. The Dunhams were among the city's prominent business families.

narrative_blocks.header: First married Rand McNally heir at nineteen
narrative_blocks.body: In October 1925, age 19, Josephine married Harry Beach Clow Jr. (1901-2000), Yale 1924 graduate and grandson of both the Rand McNally publishing dynasty AND the Clow Corporation pipe-and-valve manufacturing dynasty that built much of Chicago's sewer, water, and fire-hydrant infrastructure. Clow worked for Rand McNally from 1924, rising to vice-president and serving on the board into the 1980s; he was on the Clow Corp board 1933-1980. Known socially as 'Firecracker Harry' for his pyrotechnic party displays.

narrative_blocks.header: Married John Rodman Hooker as her second
narrative_blocks.body: By the 1934 SS Santa Rosa voyage SF→NY, Josephine had married John Rodman Hooker, her second husband. Their address by 1934 was 207 El Cerrito Ave, San Mateo CA. By 1955 they had moved to 15650 Arnold Rd, Sonoma.

burial.plot_notes: Cremated. Ashes scattered over the desert outside of Palm Springs CA, privately (per Tony Hooker pedigree).

birth.historical_notes: Per SEC filing 2013 + Tony Hooker pedigree. Likely born SF given parents' SF residence pattern.

sources.short: Ashfield Capital Partners — Form ADV Part 2B Brochure Supplement (Anthony Hooker)
Tony Hooker pedigree (#1002 Anthony Shreve Hooker)
Sam Talcott Hooker family knowledge — uncle relationship confirmed

education.notes: Per SEC filing: attended Harvard 1956-1957; left without completing degree. His father John Rodman Hooker (HD3243) was Harvard A.B. 1925.
Per SEC filing: 1966-1967, graduate work without completing degree.

Per SEC filing: M.F.A. 1995. The art-historical pivot late in his career may echo the Hooker family's artistic + scientific pattern (great-aunt Marian Osgood Hooker H04280 the photographer-physician; mother Josephine Dunham's Chicago social arts circle).

careers.notes: Per SEC filing 2013. Office: 750 Battery Street, 6th Floor, SF CA 94111.
Per SEC filing 2013: "Senior Advisor 2009 – Present". Sam confirms ongoing role.

narrative_blocks.header: Carries his Shreve grandfather's surname as middle
narrative_blocks.body: Tony was born in 1938 to John Rodman Hooker and his second wife Josephine Dunham. His middle name 'Shreve' honors his maternal grandfather George Choate Shreve, founder of Shreve & Co., considered San Francisco's oldest commercial establishment.

narrative_blocks.header: USNA 1961 then SF Art Institute MFA
narrative_blocks.body: He graduated the US Naval Academy with a B.S. in 1961 after a year at Harvard. Three decades later he earned an M.F.A. from the San Francisco Art Institute in 1995, the arts-and-finance dual track distinguishing him among Bay Area money managers.

narrative_blocks.header: Portfolio Manager at Ashfield Capital Partners since 1986
narrative_blocks.body: Since 1986 Tony has been Principal and Portfolio Manager at Ashfield Capital Partners in San Francisco, holding the Senior Advisor role since 2009. His decades there overlapped with the 2014 arrival of his godson Lyman Domning Howard.

narrative_blocks.header: Godfather to distant cousin Lyman Howard
narrative_blocks.body: He has served as godfather to Lyman Domning Howard (b. 1969), a distant Hooker cousin descended through Bessie Augusta Hooker Lent. Lyman joined Tony's firm Ashfield Capital Partners as Portfolio Manager in 2014.

narrative_blocks.header: Steward of three generations of Hooker pedigree
narrative_blocks.body: He has assembled and preserves much of the modern Hooker pedigree, drawing on family papers and his own research. The pedigree work has been a primary source for genealogical research connecting separate cousin branches.

cross_connections.display_label: Godfather to distant cousin who joined his Ashfield firm.
Carries grandmother's surname Shreve as his middle name

naming_inspiration.description: Middle name Shreve is his paternal grandmother's maiden surname, preserved in his name per the family naming pattern. His father John Rodman Hooker (HD3243) was Elizabeth's son.

death.note: Per Tony Hooker pedigree + Sam's data: lived Hillsborough San Mateo County ~10 years before death; lived San Mateo CA in 1939

burial.plot_notes: Cremated at Cypress Lawn Memorial Park Colma but NOT INTERRED there — disposition of cremains uncertain. Per Findagrave: 'Per Cypress Lawn Records, Robert Hooker was cremated at their facility but not interred there. You can call the office at 650-755-0580 to inquire of the disposition of cremains. Perhaps these children of Charles are in the family Mausoleum???'

careers.employer: Hooker & Lent (Robert G. Hooker + George H. Lent)
careers.location: 14 Post Street, San Francisco CA (telephone: Private Exchange 120)
careers.notes: Real estate agency, house brokerage, rent collection, and insurance.

Real estate and investment firm. Founded with brother-in-law George Heydenfeldt Lent (X02298, husband of his sister Bessie H04268) and brother-in-law Eugene Lent. The firm operated through at least 1932 per SF Real Estate Board Yearbook; building referenced in 1916 SF Commercial Club records. Robert G. Hooker was a Pacific-Union Club member (1932 roster) — same as his Lent business partners.
Per 1880s SF directory: 'Hooker Robert G., cashier Am. Sugar Refinery Co., r. 917 Bush' — early-career position before founding Hooker & Lent.

memberships.notes: One of the most exclusive private clubs in the western US. Robert was a member alongside his Lent business partners (his sister Bessie's husband George Heydenfeldt Lent and brother-in-law Eugene Lent).
One of the most exclusive private clubs in the western US. Per SFgenealogy 1932 roster.
Per Bohemian Club Officers & Members 1892 roster: 'HOOKER, Robert G.' — alongside brother G. Osgood Hooker and brother-in-law George H. Lent (married Bessie that same year).

Per Burlingame Country Club roster: 'HOOKER. ROBERT G.. Cunard Bldg.. 503 Market St.. San Francisco. Calif.' — listed alongside several other Hooker family members including 'Lent Hooker' (likely Rodman Lent Hooker HD3246), 'John R. Hooker' (likely John Rodman Hooker HD3243 at Curran & Dwyer), 'Osgood Hooker' (likely Osgood Hooker H05205 — C. Osgood's son), 'Mrs. C. Osgood Hooker' (Ella Wall Goodfellow Hooker, his sister-in-law widow), and 'Robert G. Hooker Jr.' (660 Market Street, Gladding McBean & Co).

narrative_blocks.header: Real estate broker with two brothers-in-law
narrative_blocks.body: He partnered with George and Eugene Lent in the Hooker & Lent firm at 503 Market Street in San Francisco. The firm operated through 1932 and built a long-running family-business network.

narrative_blocks.header: Studied mechanics at Berkeley, ended in real estate
narrative_blocks.body: He took a Ph.B. in Mechanics at the University of California, Berkeley. After early years as cashier at the American Sugar Refining Company, he settled into real estate brokerage.

narrative_blocks.header: Member of San Francisco's three elite clubs
narrative_blocks.body: He belonged to the Bohemian Club from 1892, the Pacific-Union Club, and the Burlingame Country Club. The Hooker family appeared on the Burlingame roster across three generations.

Three brothers-in-law ran Hooker & Lent real estate firm.

narrative_blocks.header: Three Hooker brothers, three California fortunes
narrative_blocks.body: Charles Gay arrived in San Francisco in spring 1851 and partnered with younger brother John Delos Hooker at Hooker & Co., 117–119 California Street — the hardware fortune that later funded the 100-inch Hooker Telescope at Mount Wilson. Older brother Henry Clay was simultaneously building Arizona's largest cattle empire.

narrative_blocks.header: Joined the Sons of the American Revolution
narrative_blocks.body: Charles Gay Hooker joined the Sons of the American Revolution, formally documenting his descent from a Revolutionary-era family. He died of pneumonia at age 83 on November 19, 1905, at his home at 967 Bush Street in San Francisco. Buried at Mountain View Cemetery in Oakland — the Bay Area's premier Victorian garden cemetery. (A Findagrave geocoding error lists the death location as 'San Francisco del Mar Municipality, Oaxaca, Mexico'; the actual location is San Francisco, California.)

narrative_blocks.header: A homestead chair followed him to San Francisco
narrative_blocks.body: He took a child's side chair from the Hinsdale homestead with him to San Francisco in 1851. The chair returned east after his 1905 death and now sits at the New Hampshire Historical Society in Concord, provenanced back to his great-grandfather Rev. Bunker Gay.

cross_connections.display_label: Carried his great-grandfather Bunker's Gay surname as a middle name

burial.plot_notes: Mountain View Cemetery, Oakland, Alameda County CA (per Findagrave Memorial 255641260).

careers.employer: Hooker & Co. (with brother John Delos Hooker + Isaac H. Cory)
careers.notes: Came to San Francisco in spring 1851; built fortune in hardware import. Hooker & Co. imported hardware, iron, and related goods. Partnered with brother John Delos Hooker (H02709, who later founded the Hooker Telescope at Mount Wilson Observatory).

Per Truman & Hooker partner biography (re. son Osgood): 'Charles G. Hooker, a prominent hardware merchant of California, who came to this State in 1852, and still resides in San Francisco.' Charles arrived CA 1852 — placed him in the second wave of Gold Rush migrants (1849-1852). Hardware merchandise business connects to the broader CA development boom serving miners + settlers.

landmarks.landmark_blurb: Grew up on the Hinsdale homestead before sailing to California in 1851; the family chair he took with him now sits at the New Hampshire Historical Society.

bio.extra_names: Tony Hooker pedigree records full first name as 'Arrabella' — formal name 'Arrabella Maria Celia Osgood'. EH 1909 lists her simply as 'Maria Celia (Osgood)' (using middle/Latin name in everyday use).

burial.plot_notes: Cypress Lawn Memorial Park, Colma San Mateo Co CA. Originally buried at Lone Mountain Cemetery, San Francisco — when that cemetery was decommissioned, remains were removed to Cypress Lawn (Findagrave Memorial 65583447).

sources.short: Tony Hooker pedigree (#1010 George Choate Shreve)
sources.page: b.11 May 1829 d.13 Oct 1893; 13th child, 5th son

sources.short: Wikipedia: Shreve & Co.
Shreve & Co. — 'Our Story' (company history)
Findagrave Memorial 65583447 (George Choate Shreve)

careers.notes: Founded what Tony Hooker pedigree calls 'the large manufacturing jewelry house of Geo. C. Shreve & Co., well known in the business world and largest establishment of the kind in the West.' Came to San Francisco in 1852 — among earliest gold-rush-era Yankee merchants — and built the firm into the West's premier jeweler. The firm continued post-mortem under family members; his son George Rodman Shreve (1861-1914) was senior member of Shreve, Treat & Eacret jewelers and formerly of Shreve & Co. The Shreve family remained a fixture of SF jewelry retailing into the 20th century.

notable.notable_blurb: Founded Shreve & Co., oldest SF commercial establishment
notable.primary_url_label: Wikipedia: Shreve & Co. (George has no own page; firm article serves as primary)

narrative_blocks.header: Sailed to gold-rush San Francisco with nephew
narrative_blocks.body: George Choate Shreve was born May 11, 1829 in Salem, Essex County MA — the 13th child and 5th son of Isaac Shreve (1779-1829) and Mary Moulton. His father died the same year George was born. As a young man George learned goldsmithing from his older half-brother Benjamin (b.1780, who later returned to Salem and founded Shreve, Crump & Low in Boston in 1869, a firm that still claims to be 'America's Oldest and Finest Jeweler').

narrative_blocks.header: Built largest jewelry house in the West
narrative_blocks.body: Per the Tony Hooker pedigree (which records George's brief biography from late-19th-century San Francisco mercantile sources): George was 'head of the large manufacturing jewelry house of Geo. C. Shreve & Co., well known in the business world and the largest establishment of the kind in the West.' By the 1860s the firm was considered one of the finest silver and goldsmiths in America.

narrative_blocks.header: His firm's earthquake-proof building survived 1906
narrative_blocks.body: Thirteen years after George's 1893 death, the firm — by then run by his son George Rodman Shreve and others — opened in March 1906 a brand-new eleven-story 'earthquake-proof' building at the corner of Post Street and Grant Avenue, San Francisco. One month later, the great earthquake struck. When the dust settled, the Shreve Building was one of the only structures still standing in the firm's neighborhood.

narrative_blocks.header: His daughter married into Hooker mercantile dynasty
narrative_blocks.body: In 1858 George Choate Shreve married Rebecca Rodman Nichols of Salem MA. Their daughter Elizabeth Lawton Shreve (1868-1943) married Robert Gay Hooker on April 18, 1900 — uniting two prominent SF mercantile families: the Hookers in hardware (Hooker & Co.) and the Shreves in jewelry (Geo. C.

bio.extra_names: Per Findagrave for son George Rodman Shreve, after first husband's death she remarried to a Creamer, becoming 'Rebecca Rodman Nichols Creamer'

sources.short: Tony Hooker pedigree

INST002.name: Harvard College
INST002.notes: Rev. Thomas Shepard (I00002) was instrumental in founding Harvard and placing it at Cambridge. Rev. Samuel Shepard (H00008) graduated 1658; Samuel Shepard grandson (H00038) graduated 1685; Col. Roger Newton (H00044) also a graduate.

INST094.name: Pacific-Union Club

founding_year.founder: Merger of Pacific Club (founded 1852) and Union Club (founded 1854)

location.address: 1000 California Street (Flood Mansion atop Nob Hill since 1912)

INST094.description: The Pacific-Union Club is San Francisco's oldest and most exclusive private men's club, formed in 1889 by the merger of the Pacific Club (1852) and the Union Club (1854) — both gold-rush-era gentlemen's clubs. Since 1912 it has occupied the James C. Flood Mansion atop Nob Hill at 1000 California Street, the only Nob Hill mansion to survive the 1906 earthquake and fire. Membership has historically included San Francisco's mercantile, banking, legal, and political elite. The Hooker family carried multi-generational membership: the 1905 Officers and Members directory lists three Hooker descendants — Charles Gay Hooker (H02703, address 967 Bush), C. Osgood Hooker (H04265, his son), and R.G. Hooker (Robert Gay Hooker, H04266, address 14 Post). A generation later, Charles Gay's grandson John Rodman Hooker (HD3243, Hooker & Fay senior partner) was also a member per his Golden Nugget Library biography. The Hooker presence at the club thus spans four generations — and is presumed to have continued with John Rodman's son Anthony Shreve 'Tony' Hooker.

sources.short: Wikipedia: Pacific-Union Club
Pacific-Union Club, 'Officers and Members 1905' directory

INST094.notes:
[merged from INST100]: Founded 1889 by merger of two earlier SF clubs (Pacific Club + Union Club). Houses in the historic James C. Flood Mansion atop Nob Hill — one of the few buildings to survive the 1906 earthquake. One of the most exclusive private clubs in the western US. Hooker family members documented: Eugene Lent (X02301, 1924+1932 rosters per SFgenealogy), Robert G. Hooker (H04266, 1932 roster). C. Osgood Hooker (H04265) likely also a member but specific roster confirmation pending.
[type detail: category=social_club]
[type detail: sub_category=private_club]
[research_notes] Three Hooker descendants documented as 1905 members: father Charles Gay (H02703), son C. Osgood (H04265), and son Robert Gay (H04266). The Hooker mercantile dynasty was an integral part of late-19th and early-20th-century SF business society. The Flood Mansion home of the club is itself a survivor of the 1906 earthquake — one of very few Nob Hill structures still standing — making the club a physical link to gold-rush-era San Francisco.

INST097.primary_name: Cate School (formerly The Santa Barbara School / Miss Cate's School)
INST097.name: Cate School (formerly The Santa Barbara School / Miss Cate's School)
INST097.description: Cate School, founded 1910 in Santa Barbara CA as Miss Cate's School (also known as The Santa Barbara School for Boys / The Santa Barbara School), moved to Carpinteria CA in 1932 and was rebranded Cate School. An elite West Coast college-preparatory school. HD3243 John Rodman Hooker graduated 1921 (when called The Santa Barbara School) and later served as President of the Board of Trustees of the Alumni Association. HD3315 Rodman L. Hooker Jr. attended in the 1950s under the Cate School name.

sources.short: Golden Nugget Library — John R. Hooker biography

INST097.notes:
[type detail: category=educational_institution]
[type detail: sub_category=private_preparatory_school]
[research_notes] Two documented Hooker connections: HD3243 John Rodman Hooker (graduated 1921 when school was The Santa Barbara School; later Alumni Board President) and HD3315 Rodman L. Hooker Jr. (attended in the 1950s under the Cate School name). The institutional continuity from The Santa Barbara School to Cate School (1910→1932 reorganization in Carpinteria) was confirmed by the compiler in April 2026.

INST098.name: Burlingame Country Club

founding_year.founder: San Francisco's mercantile and railroad elite

INST098.description: Burlingame Country Club, founded 1893 in what is now Hillsborough San Mateo County, was the first country club established west of the Mississippi. Membership has historically drawn from San Francisco's banking, railroad, and mercantile elite. John Rodman Hooker (HD3243, Hooker & Fay brokerage senior partner) was a member per his Golden Nugget Library biography. His Hillsborough/San Mateo residence pattern — also recorded for his father Robert Gay Hooker's Hillsborough address per Sam Talcott Hooker's father's obituary — overlapped with Burlingame Country Club's geographic and social orbit.

sources.short: Wikipedia: Burlingame Country Club
Golden Nugget Library — John R. Hooker biography

INST098.notes:
[merged from INST101]: Founded 1893; private country club in Hillsborough San Mateo County CA. The club's roster (year unknown) lists multiple members of the SF Hooker family clan — making it the densest single-institution Hooker-family roster outside immediate family functions: 'Mrs. C. Osgood Hooker' (Ella Wall Goodfellow Hooker, widow of H04265, Eucalyptus Road Burlingame); 'Osgood Hooker' (H05205, son of C. Osgood, 251 Kearny St SF); 'John R. Hooker' (likely HD3243 John Rodman Hooker, Kohl Bldg 486 California St, w/Curran & Dwyer); 'Robert G. Hooker' (H04266, Cunard Bldg 503 Market St); 'Robert G. Hooker Jr.' (Robert's son, 660 Market St, w/Gladding McBean & Co.); 'Lent Hooker' (likely Rodman Lent Hooker HD3246, 257 El Cerrito Ave San Mateo). Six Hooker-family members at the same club shows how thoroughly the family integrated into San Mateo Peninsula elite society.
[type detail: category=social_club]
[type detail: sub_category=country_club]
[research_notes] 1 documented Hooker member: HD3243 John Rodman Hooker. Future research may identify additional Hooker family members from the Hillsborough Robert Gay Hooker line (Sam Talcott Hooker's grandfather Rodman Lent Hooker likely lived in the same area).

crossConnections.display_label: Named in honor of his telescope-funding great-uncle.
